title: Effects of adaptation to altered color statistics provide evidence for calibration of color perception to the color statistics of natural scenes.
llm_reason: This paper concerns perceptual adaptation in color vision and efficient coding theory, not decision making under risk, uncertainty, or cognitive evaluation of choice options.

title: Visual working memory for real-world objects is resistant to visual but vulnerable to semantic interference.
Risky choices over goods
llm_reason: The paper directly investigates individual-level risk preferences and how the format of rewards (goods vs. monetary credit) affects risky choice behavior, touching on representation of options and utility under uncertainty. It uses controlled experiments to isolate cognitive/psychological factors driving deviations from normative predictions.

title: Reference points, risk-taking behavior, and competitive outcomes in sequential settings
llm_reason: The paper examines individual-level risk-taking decisions under competitive pressure in a sequential setting, directly engaging with how reference points and rivalry shape choice behavior under uncertainty. It addresses heterogeneity in risk responses and counterfactual outcomes, which connects to individual decision-making processes under risk.

title: Uncertainty (ambiguity) aversion and seeking
llm_reason: The paper directly addresses ambiguity aversion and seeking, which are core topics in individual decision making under uncertainty, including how people evaluate and respond to unknown probabilities—a central concern of cognitive and process-oriented research.

title: Learning from the storm: The impact of hurricane experience on future disaster preparedness
llm_reason: The paper focuses on household-level field behavior and preparedness decisions using observational purchase data without modeling individual cognitive processes, attention, or decision mechanisms. It lacks process-level or formal cognitive modeling components central to the research agenda.

title: Does signed prediction error drive declarative memory? Evidence from variable choice paradigms
llm_reason: The paper focuses on prediction error and declarative memory consolidation in a learning paradigm, not on individual decision making under risk, uncertainty, or complexity. While it involves reward and choice, the primary research question concerns memory encoding rather than decision processes.

title: Different sources of the numerical comparison size effect
llm_reason: The paper uses diffusion model decomposition to investigate decision threshold adjustment and process-level parameters (response times, accuracy) in numerical comparison tasks, directly addressing cognitive mechanisms underlying decision making. It concerns evidence accumulation models and how stimulus properties influence decision processes.

title: Spotlight on the past: Focusing attention on long-term memory
llm_reason: This paper focuses on memory retrieval mechanisms and attentional processes in cognitive psychology, not on decision making under risk, uncertainty, or choice complexity. It does not address any of the core topics of the research agenda such as evaluation of choice options, risk preferences, or decision processes.

title: Cognitive constraints and reward environments jointly shape memory formation.
llm_reason: The paper presents a computational model of memory formation under cognitive constraints and reward environments, directly addressing how cognitive resources are strategically allocated—highly relevant to process-level, cognitively-oriented decision-making research. It models gain-sensitive encoding, metacognitive control, and optimal adaptation under limited resources, all central to the PhD student's agenda.

title: The role of attention in framing: How question valence attenuates attribute framing bias.
